observation_meta.capture_context: email viewer in mobile email client (likely iOS Mail or similar)

page_identity.purpose_summary: Display an official announcement email from OpenAI about the release of GPT-5.5, including usage instructions and migration guidance.

page_overview.layout_summary: Vertical scrollable email body with standard email header (sender, recipient, timestamp), centered brand logo, multi-paragraph rich text body, and bottom action bar. Clean white background, minimal UI chrome.
page_overview.high_level_semantics: Informational broadcast email; no form inputs, no dynamic state toggles; purely declarative content presentation with embedded hyperlinks and command snippet.

children.text: We're excited to introduce GPT-5.5, our smartest and most intuitive to use model yet, and the next step toward a new way of getting work done on a computer. GPT-5.5 is now available in the API, Codex, and ChatGPT.

GPT-5.5 understands what you're trying to do faster and can carry more of the work itself. It also uses significantly fewer tokens to complete the same tasks, making it more efficient as well as more capable.

To get the most out of GPT-5.5 in the API, check out our API prompt guide and migration guide, or use the command: [$openai-docs migrate this project to gpt-5.5] to kick off a plan to migrate your API integration via Codex.
GPT-5.5 is now the default model in Codex

structural_blocks.description: Email metadata section (subject, sender, recipient, timestamp)
Main rich-text announcement with brand logo and 4 paragraphs
Fixed bottom action bar with 4 standard email actions

text_elements: GPT-5.5 is now the default model in Codex

interaction_clues: BackButton → likely returns to email list/inbox
HideButton → toggles visibility of sender proxy details
Bottom action bar: Delete, Share, Reply/Forward, More — standard email operations
Hyperlinked text: 'prompt guide', 'migration guide' — likely tappable links (no underline visible but semantic intent clear)
Command snippet in brackets — not interactive, purely illustrative

navigation_hints.return_path: BackButton → previous screen (email list or folder)
navigation_hints.main_cta: None explicit; primary user action likely: read → possibly tap 'prompt guide' or 'migration guide'

state_hints.read_state: unread? (no visual indicator like dot or bold subject)

overlay_hints.trigger_source: system-level translation feature (likely OS or email app)
overlay_hints.dismiss_method: tap X icon or tap outside (X visible)
overlay_hints.content_summary: Translate Email (Chinese label), with green 'A 文' icon

implementation_semantics.layout_pattern: Standard email detail layout: header + scrollable body + fixed bottom bar
visual_blocks: Centered brand logo as visual anchor
Paragraph blocks with consistent leading and indentation
Monospaced command snippet using bracket delimiters
Floating action with dismiss X (common in iOS/Android mail apps)
implementation_semantics.styling_hints: Sans-serif body text, bold for key terms ('introduce GPT-5.5'), underlined links (implied by semantics), dark gray secondary text for metadata

significant_elements: Command snippet exactly: [$openai-docs migrate this project to gpt-5.5]
Floating '翻译邮件' button with green A文 icon and X
key_facts: Email is from OpenAI, dated future (2026) — likely mock/test data
GPT-5.5 announced as available in API, Codex, ChatGPT
GPT-5.5 claimed as default in Codex
No images, only text and icons
uncertainties: Whether 'prompt guide' and 'migration guide' are actual tappable links (no underline visible, but semantic expectation strong)
Exact behavior of '隐藏' button — may toggle full sender details or just proxy line
Whether translation overlay is system-level or app-specific
Whether bottom action bar is native OS or app-implemented
